title: Ablation studies
scope: Add/remove feature groups, measure impact. Explains how to systemically drop subsets of features to measure their aggregate impact on the model's performance.
key_concepts: An ablation study evaluates feature importance by removing components and observing the performance drop.
Features are typically removed in logical groups (e.g., all demographic features, all text embeddings) rather than one by one, especially in high-dimensional spaces.
A significant performance drop indicates the removed group holds high predictive utility.
definitions: Ablation study: An experimental procedure where specific components or feature groups are removed from a machine learning system to evaluate their contribution.
examples: Removing a block of historical transaction features and retraining the model. If AUC drops by 5%, the historical transactions carry strong signal.
relations_to_other_chapters: Prepares the ground for specific importance metrics (s04, s05) which look at features individually.
Ties into evaluating complex feature construction (Ch 6).
what_to_exclude: Neural network layer ablation (focus purely on feature ablation).
